sections.paragraph:
θεώρημα, ατος, τό (in var. mngs. since Pla., Aristot., Demosth., also En 19:3 [‘vision’]; Philo) someth. seen, a sight καινόν σοι ἔχω εἰπεῖν θ. I want to tell you about someth. unusual that I saw AcPl Ox 6, 1f (διήγημα Aa I, 241, 11).—DELG s.v. θεωρός.

var. var. = various(ly)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

AcPl Ox 6 AcPl Ox 6 = Acts of Paul, POxy 6—List 1

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 454
